architecture.summary: A constrained decoding method for autoregressive language models that modifies token logits to exclude undesirable tokens while minimizing KL divergence from the model's original conditional probability distribution. The system reformulates naive logit masking (setting banned token logits to −∞) as a KL divergence minimization problem, re-normalizing allowed token probabilities according to Equation (3) to preserve the intrinsic distribution. The logit processor applies a transformation (Equation 7) that sets banned token logits to −∞ then re-normalizes remaining logits, yielding a posterior distribution over allowed tokens that minimizes distributional distortion.

biblio.title: (G)I-DLE: Generative Inference via Distribution-preserving Logit Exclusion with KL Divergence Minimization for Constrained Decoding

claims.statement: Naive logit masking, which sets the logits of banned tokens to −∞, induces a re-normalized distribution that distorts the original probability mass of allowed tokens and increases output variance.
On Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct, (G)I-DLE achieves higher mean score (4.966667) compared to baseline generation (4.911111).
On Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct, (G)I-DLE substantially reduces variance from 0.216729 (baseline) to 0.055056.
On Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, (G)I-DLE achieves mean score of 4.900000 with variance 0.113483, compared to baseline mean 4.716667 and variance 0.517135.
On Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, (G)I-DLE achieves mean score 4.555556 compared to baseline 4.483333.
On Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct, (G)I-DLE achieves mean score 3.455556 compared to baseline 3.266667.
Naive masked generation produces only marginal improvements in mean score over baseline while exhibiting higher variance, particularly for smaller models.

(G)I-DLE consistently achieves higher mean evaluation scores with substantially reduced variance across Qwen2.5 models ranging from 1.5B to 14B, producing more fluent, logically coherent, and stable outputs.
